def: s.m. The eleventh solar month (when the sun is in Aquarius); — name of Ardshīr, son of Isfandyār; — name of a plant which flowers in the month bahman (it is distinguished, from the colour of the root, into two kinds, surḵẖ, 'red,' and sufaid 'white').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بهمن (bahman), ultimately from Old Persian *Vahumanah (literally “good mind”).

v.n. To flow; to go or swim with the stream; to float; to drift; to glide, slide; to run (as a sore); to blow (as a breeze); to pass, drive; to be carried away, be separated; to be lost, ruined, or destroyed; to be wasted, squandered, or dissipated; to melt, dissolve, become watery
subs: v.n. = bahnā (esp. in an intens. sense); to run off or away; to blow away, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀳𑀤𑀺 (vahadi), from Sanskrit वहति (váhati).

(-se), To be in possession (of), to participate, share; to derive benefit (from); to accomplish an object, be fortunate, to prosper

bah-rūp, adj. & s.m. s.f. One of the seven tongues of fire
adj. & s.m. = bahu-rūp, and bahu-rūpiyā, qq.v.

s.m. Mimicry: bahu-rekhā, s.m. Many lines, wrinkles, furrows, marks of care or pain or trouble
s.f. A woman who has given birth to many children
adj. Having many branches or ramifications
s.m. A long while, a length of time

adj. & s.m. Many-rooted; — the grass or reed Hyperanthera moringa

s.m. lit. 'Possessing much rice'; (in Gram.) A relative or adjective compound (a class of compounds in which the last member, being a substantive, loses its independence as well as its original grammatical character, and together with the first member serves only to qualify or define another word; the words so formed are usually called epithets; e.g. ćatur-bhuj, 'the four-armed,' an epithet of Vishnu. The word bahu-vrīhi itself is an example of this rule)
adj. & s.m. Containing much seed, having many seeds; — the custard-apple, Annona squamosa

adj. & s.m. Containing much seed ( = bahu-vīj); — the plant Terminalia bellerica; the plant Amaranthus polygonoides; the plant Bombax heptaphyllum; the plant Vangueria spinosa; name of several other plants.
